title: alchemilla vulgaris f. subcrenata
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris f. subcrenata, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rosaceae family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and rounded, toothed leaves. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties also provide several health benefits, including reducing menstrual cramps and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-aging effects. Alchemilla vulgaris f. subcrenata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and dried leaves, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
